acceptance_targets: [AWARD_SECOND_TURN_MISSING] Pass only if at least one beat after the fall creates a new on-page consequence beyond the current false report and craving sequence. Fail if the revised chapter still reaches the same understanding by the report/comedown section that it had already reached once Margot gives the altitude explanation.

[CRAFT_PATTERN_SETUP_UNDERPLANTED] Pass only if a cold reader can point to at least two distinct on-page cues before chapter end that Margot has done this before and that Petra's later review would uncover repeated abuse rather than a one-off threshold mistake. One cue may be the current manual edit; at least one additional cue must be present in the revised text.
[RED-004] Ch4 does not re-state Tomas's age. His Quito family context is conveyed through action or stakes, not as biographical re-introduction.
[RED-006] Ch4 coaching suite entry does not re-inventory wall monitors, chair description, or processing hardware as spatial information.
[RED-024] Ch4 does not repeat the phrase 'third floor of the residential wing' or any near-verbatim echo of Ch2's quarter-entry template.
[length_chapter_04_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 3500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if at least one beat after the fall creates a new on-page consequence beyond the current false report and craving sequence. Fail if the revised chapter still reaches the same understanding by the report/comedown section that it had already reached once Margot gives the altitude explanation.
findings.problem: The chapter lands the expected plot obligations, but once Tomas falls it mostly certifies information the reader already has: Margot lies, the paperwork can hide the edit, and she wants another feed. The back half therefore lacks a sharper second turn or new liability, so the chapter reads as efficient setup rather than a fully realized seduction-concealment chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the post-fall progression at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:79; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:123; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:141; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:157 so a later beat creates new pressure instead of restating the existing one. Keep the injury, concealment, and comedown, but add one irreversible choice or concrete new liability after the fall: a challenged diagnosis that forces a deeper lie, a logged discrepancy that heightens exposure, or a near-relapse that materially changes what the next sessions will mean.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can point to at least two distinct on-page cues before chapter end that Margot has done this before and that Petra's later review would uncover repeated abuse rather than a one-off threshold mistake. One cue may be the current manual edit; at least one additional cue must be present in the revised text.
findings.problem: The tracked setup `margot_feed_pattern` is underplanted. The context pack says this chapter must make clear that Margot routinely pushes athletes past safe heart-rate margins and that the pattern is visible in telemetry, but the page currently shows only a single target bump plus a vague audit mention. As written, Chapter 15 would be discovering one incident and calling it a pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:63; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:65; and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:149, add at least one additional concrete sign that this is practiced recurring behavior. Options: Margot recognizes the movement as habitual, mentally compares Tomas's numbers to prior overpushes, notices earlier hidden edits in her own queue, or calibrates how far she can raise a target without triggering immediate scrutiny. Keep the scene local to Tomas, but make the later auditable pattern legible on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch4 does not re-state Tomas's age. His Quito family context is conveyed through action or stakes, not as biographical re-introduction.
findings.evidence: Ch3 line 413: 'Tomás Reyes arrived at seven thirty, twenty-one, Ecuadorian, first Apex cycle'; Ch4 line 614: 'Tomás was twenty-one and eager and his family in Quito needed him to be good'
findings.problem: Adjacent-chapter re-introduction of Tomas's age and identity. Ch3 introduces him as twenty-one, Ecuadorian, first Apex cycle. Ch4 re-states his age and adds family-in-Quito context one chapter later. The reader met him in Ch3; Ch4 re-teaches who he is.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch4 line 614, cut 'twenty-one and' — the reader knows his age from one chapter ago. The family-in-Quito motivation can stay if trimmed to the need itself rather than the identity package.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch4 coaching suite entry does not re-inventory wall monitors, chair description, or processing hardware as spatial information.
findings.evidence: Ch2 line 264: 'neural coaching suite, small and windowless, with two reclining stations, a wall of displays… Processing hardware hummed behind the panels'; Ch4 line 582: 'coaching suite smelled like adhesive tape and the faint metallic discharge of processing hardware running warm'; Ch4 line 586: 'Three monitors on the wall held empty biometric grids. The chair was padded and built for long sessions'
findings.problem: Coaching suite re-described in Ch4 after establishment in Ch2. Both entries inventory the processing hardware and wall displays. Two chapters apart; reader already knows the room.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch4, cut the spatial inventory (monitors, chair description). Enter through Margot's anticipatory state — already docked, watching the green ready-light, mouth going dry — which is the chapter's actual opening move.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch4 does not repeat the phrase 'third floor of the residential wing' or any near-verbatim echo of Ch2's quarter-entry template.
findings.evidence: Ch2 line 326: 'quarters on the third floor of the residential wing: single bed, desk, bathroom, window facing the ridge'; Ch4 line 706: 'Her quarters were on the third floor of the residential wing, facing the mountain'
findings.problem: Near-verbatim spatial template repeated across chapters. 'Quarters on the third floor of the residential wing' appears in both Ch2 (Lena) and Ch4 (Margot) as an orienting sentence. The 10-word sequence is an exact match. The reader already knows where the residential wing is.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch4, cut the 'third floor of the residential wing' location tag. Enter Margot's room through what she sees or feels — the mountain view, the cold glass — not through the building's floor plan.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 3500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
